topic: Agentic AI for reliable academic literature review and hallucination mitigation in large language models
final_review:
The integration of Agentic AI in academic literature review has the potential to enhance the reliability and efficiency of the process (Kasneci et al., 2023). However, one of the significant challenges associated with large language models is hallucination, which can lead to the generation of inaccurate or misleading information (Dwivedi et al., 2023). To mitigate this issue, it is essential to develop explainable artificial intelligence (XAI) models that can provide transparent and interpretable results (Barredo Arrieta et al., 2019).

The application of AI in higher education has been increasingly explored, with a focus on its potential to improve learning outcomes and educational experiences (Zawacki-Richter et al., 2019). The use of large language models also raises concerns about academic dishonesty and the need for new assessment methods (Rudolph et al., 2023). In this context, the development of Agentic AI models that can facilitate reliable academic literature review and mitigate hallucination is crucial.

Recent studies have highlighted the opportunities and challenges associated with the use of large language models in education (Kasneci et al., 2023; Dwivedi et al., 2023). For instance, large language models have been shown to have potential applications in education, but their limitations and biases need to be carefully considered (Grassini, 2023). A multidisciplinary approach that involves educators, researchers, and policymakers is essential to address these challenges (Dwivedi et al., 2019).

In conclusion, the development of Agentic AI models for reliable academic literature review and hallucination mitigation is a critical area of research. By leveraging the potential of large language models and addressing their limitations, more efficient and effective academic literature review processes can be created (Feuerriegel et al., 2023).

References:
Barredo Arrieta, A., Díaz-Rodríguez, N., Del Ser, J. (2019)
Dwivedi, Y. K., Hughes, L., Ismagilova, E. (2019)

Grassini, S. (2023)

Rudolph, J., Tan, S., Tan, S. (2023)
Zawacki-Richter, O., Marín, V. I., Bond, M. (2019)
